title: Validate MCP tool inputs against allowlists before spawning subprocesses
source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 — LLM06 Excessive Agency & LLM08 (tool boundary). mcp_server.py delegate/meeseeks tools pass raw inputs to subprocess args and log filenames.
description: mcp_server.py:751 `delegate()` passes the LLM-supplied `ticket_id` and `agent` straight into subprocess args and interpolates `ticket_id` into a log filename (`f"delegate-{ticket_id}-...log"`) with no validation. A ticket_id containing path separators or `..` causes log-path traversal, and an arbitrary `agent` string is forwarded unchecked. The MCP tool surface is the trust boundary where the model's output becomes execution, so inputs must be validated there, not only deep inside delegate.py.
proposed_changes: In mcp_server.py, import sanitize_ticket_id / SAFE_ID_PATTERN from security_utils and validate at the top of delegate(), meeseeks(), and any tool that forwards user-controlled IDs: reject ticket_id via sanitize_ticket_id() (raises on traversal), and validate `agent` against a fixed AGENT_ROLES allowlist set. Sanitize every value interpolated into log filenames through sanitize_path_component() before building the path passed to _spawn_detached(). Return a structured {"error": ...} JSON on rejection and audit_log_security_event() the rejection.
